commands.name: login
commands.description: Authenticate with Postman to access your resources.

flags.description: Specify the alias of the API key to fetch your resources using it. (This option is discontinued and will be removed in a future release.)
Specify the region for authentication. Use "eu" for EU region.

commands.name: logout
commands.description: Delete the stored Postman API key.

flags.description: Specify the alias of the API key to fetch your resources using it. (This option is discontinued and will be removed in a future release.)

commands.name: collection
commands.description: Run and test your Postman collections directly from the command line.

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: Migrate a v2.1 collection to the v3 format

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.description: Run linting on a local v3 collection at the given file or directory path.

flags.description: Exit with failure if diagnostics at this level or above are present. [choices: "error", "warning"] (default: "error")

subcommands.name: ai-readiness
subcommands.description: Score a Postman collection for AI readiness by ID, local file path, or local-mode directory.

flags.description: Exit with a non-zero code if the overall score is below this threshold (0-100).

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Initiate a Postman collection run from a given ID or path.

flags.description: Specify an Id or path to a Postman Environment
Specify an Id or path to a file containing Postman Globals
Specify the reporters to use for the run: cli,json,junit,html. For multiple reporters, provide their names as a comma-separated list (E.g., -r cli,json) (default: ["cli"])

[Optional] Specify a path to save the report. By default, reports are saved to the /postman-cli-reports directory in your current working directory. If the directory doesn't exist, it will be created automatically. If the specified path is an existing directory, the report file will be saved within it. Supported reporters: json, junit and html.
[Optional] Generate JSON report using the Newman schema. By default, JSON reports use the native structure of Postman CLI.
[Optional] Remove all request bodies from the report. Supported reporters: json, html.
[Optional] Remove all response bodies from the report. Supported reporters: json, html.
[Optional] Remove all request and response headers from the report. Supported reporters: json, html.
[Optional] Remove all request and response headers, as well as request and response bodies, from the report. Supported reporters: json, html.
Define the number of iterations to run
Specify a data file to use for iterations (either JSON or CSV)
[BETA] Path to a .dataset.yaml whose view drives the iteration data. Mutually exclusive with --iteration-data. Requires --iteration-data-view.
[BETA] Name or id of the view to execute (within --iteration-data-dataset). The view's result set drives one iteration per row.

[BETA] Pass --dataset multiple times to load several .dataset.yaml files (or directories containing them). Each is made available to scripts as pm.datasets(<id>). When provided, auto-discovery of .dataset.yaml files from the collection's parent repo is disabled. (default: [])

Specify the request/folder id, name, or path to run from the collection. Use a path (e.g. "FolderName/RequestName") to disambiguate items with the same name. Can be specified multiple times to run multiple items (default: [])
Allows the specification of global variables via the command line, in a key=value format
Allows the specification of environment variables via the command line, in a key=value format (default: [])
Specify the integration id for the report. (Only supported in the US region)
API Key used to load the resources from the Postman API (Only supported in the US region, use 'postman login --region' to authenticate instead)
Specify the alias of the API key to fetch remote resources using it.
Specify whether or not to gracefully stop a collection run on encountering an error and whether to end the run with an error based on the optional modifier
Prevents Postman CLI from automatically following 3XX redirect responses
Specify whether or not to override the default exit code for the current run
Prevents Postman CLI from showing output to CLI
Forces Unicode compliant symbols to be replaced by their plain text equivalents
Specify the extent of delay between requests (milliseconds) (default: 0)
Specify a timeout for collection run (milliseconds) (default: 0)
Specify the path to the working directory
Prevents reading the files situated outside of the working directory
Specify the path to a client certificates configurations (JSON)
Specify the path to a client certificate (PEM)
Specify the path to a client certificate private key
Specify the client certificate passphrase (for protected key)
Specify the path to a custom cookie jar (serialized tough-cookie JSON)
Exports the cookie jar to a file after completing the run
Show detailed information of collection run and each request sent
Start a mock server from manifest (.json) file
Start mock servers with fault-injection scenarios from a .sim.yaml file

commands.name: api
commands.description: Publish and test your APIs directly from the command line

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.description: Run linting on the schema of the given API ID or local file in the given path. If [api] is not provided we will run the linting on the schema present in the .postman config file

flags.description: Specify the integration ID for the report.
Results of this level or above will trigger a failure exit code. [choices: "error", "warn", "info", "hint"] (default: "ERROR")
Specify whether or not to override the default exit code for the current run

subcommands.name: publish
subcommands.description: Publish a version of an API for the given apiId. All entities added to the API will be published by default. User can choose which entities to publish by using additional options.

flags.description: Specify the name of the version to publish.
Specify release notes for the version to publish.
Specify the collections to publish. If API is connected to a repository, provide file path instead of IDs.

Specify the API Definition to publish. If API is connected to a repository, provide schema root file path. Note that some repository connections utilize a schema directory path; for such APIs, provide schema directory path instead of file path.
Specify whether or not to poll for completion status of the publish action.
Specify whether or not to override the default exit code for the current run.

commands.name: runner
commands.description: Run runners on your own environments for monitoring your APIs

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start a runner

flags.description: The ID of the runner to execute
The secret key for the runner
Specify the region for the runner. Use "eu" for EU region.
External proxy URL (e.g., http://proxy.com or http://proxy.com:8080)
Custom egress proxy authorization service base URL
Additional trusted CA certificates (PEM file, can contain multiple certs)
Enable the metrics server for health checks

commands.name: spec
commands.description: Lint and validate Specifications from the command line

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.description: Run linting on the given specification by ID or local file path.

flags.description: Results of this level or above will trigger a failure exit code. [choices: "error", "warning", "info", "hint"] (default: "ERROR")
The workspace ID to use for fetching governance rulesets.

subcommands.name: ai-readiness
subcommands.description: Score an OpenAPI specification for AI readiness by ID or local file path.

flags.description: Exit with a non-zero code if the overall score is below this threshold (0-100).

commands.name: monitor
commands.description: Invoke a monitor run and display results

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Invoke a monitor run and display results.

flags.description: Specify whether or not to override the default exit code for the current run
Maximum wait time for run completion in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes) (default: 900000)

subcommands.name: prepare
subcommands.description: Prepare local collections and environments for push by validating and regenerating IDs if needed.

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: Push local collection, environment, specification, mock, and simulation changes to Postman workspace.

flags.description: Skip ID validation and auto-fix before pushing

commands.name: performance
commands.description: Manage performance tests on your collections.

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run a performance test on a collection

flags.description: Specify an Id to a Postman Environment
Specify an Id to a Postman Globals
Duration of the performance test in minutes (default: 10)
Load profile type: fixed, ramp-up, spike, peak (default: "ramp-up")
Path to a JSON or CSV data file to use with the collection
API Key used to load the resources from the Postman API (Only supported in the US region, use 'postman login --region' to authenticate instead)

Pass condition in format: function(metric, value). Functions: less_than, greater_than, less_than_eq, greater_than_eq. Metrics: avg, p90, p95, p99, error_rate, rps. Example: less_than(p95, 500) or less_than(error_rate, 5)

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all flows

flags.description: Specify the workspace ID to list flows from
Filter flows by name. Pattern may be a regex, or a name prefix.
Sort flows by criteria: "name" or "updated" (default: updated) (default: "updated")

subcommands.name: trigger
subcommands.description: Trigger a deployed flow

flags.description: Set key=value pairs in the trigger payload (e.g. --input foo=bar --input baz=qux) (default: [])
Set payload values from JSON files (e.g. --input-file ./inputs.json --input-file ./config.json) (default: [])
Set query parameters as key=value pairs (e.g. --query foo=bar --query baz=qux) (default: [])
Set custom headers as key=value pairs (e.g. --headers X-API-Key=12345 --headers User-Agent=MyApp) (default: [])
Show the flow requestURL and payload that would be sent without making the actual request (default: false)
Show actual authentication tokens in dry-run output (otherwise secrets are masked) (default: false)
Show only the response body (default: false)
Use a named scenario from the flow definition to construct the input payload, headers, and query parameters (overridden by --headers/--query)

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploy a flow (required to trigger it)

flags.description: Specify the URL path for the trigger
Specify the HTTP session timeout (5000ms to 60000ms) (default: "10000ms")
Enable authentication for the trigger (default: false)

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run a single flow from a file (e.g. ./path/to/flow.json)

flags.description: Set flow inputs as key=value pairs (e.g. --input foo=bar --input baz=qux) (default: [])
Set flow inputs from JSON files (e.g. --input-file ./inputs.json --input-file ./config.json) (default: [])
Use a pre-built scenario as flow inputs (by scenario name). Can be combined with --input and --input-file to override values.
Specify a path to a Postman Environment file (JSON or YAML)
Specify the path to the working directory
Show detailed information of flow run and each request (method, URL, assertions)
Show full output without truncating long values
Save execution result to a file (supported: json)
Save a test results report to a file (supported: html)
Specify whether or not to override the default exit code for the current run
Workspace ID required for flows that contain connector blocks. Find it in the Postman app URL: https://postman.com/workspace/<workspaceId>

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update settings for a deployed flow

flags.description: Enable or disable the flow trigger
Enable or disable authentication for the flow trigger

subcommands.name: list-runs
subcommands.description: List run history for a deployed flow

subcommands.name: get-run
subcommands.description: Analyze a specific flow run

flags.description: Specify the run ID to analyze
Filter event log by block ID prefix (can be specified multiple times) (default: [])

commands.name: request
commands.description: Send HTTP requests from the command line with full Postman capabilities including authentication, environment variables, assertions, retries, and detailed output control.

flags.description: Add HTTP header in key:value format. Can be used multiple times. Example: -H "Content-Type:application/json" -H "X-API-Key:abc123" (default: [])
Request body content. Supports: inline string, @filepath for file content, or "-" for stdin. Example: --body '{"name":"John"}' or --body @data.json
Add multipart/form-data field in key=value format. Can be used multiple times. For file uploads use key=@filepath. Example: -f "name=John" -f "avatar=@photo.jpg" (default: [])
Load Postman Environment by UUID or file path. Variables in URL, headers, and body will be resolved. Example: -e dev.postman_environment.json

[Optional] Supply authentication parameters dynamically. Use --auth-<type>-<param> where <type> is the auth method and <param> is the parameter. Supported auth types: basic, bearer, digest, oauth1, oauth2, hawk, aws, ntlm, apikey Examples: Basic Auth: --auth-basic-username user --auth-basic-password pass Bearer Token: --auth-bearer-token mytoken123 API Key: --auth-apikey-key "X-API-Key" --auth-apikey-value "abc123" --auth-apikey-in header AWS: --auth-aws-accessKey KEY --auth-aws-secretKey SECRET --auth-aws-service s3 Type: string, Default: null
Request timeout in milliseconds. Request will fail if no response within this time. (default: 300000)
Prevents Postman CLI from automatically following 3XX redirect responses. By default, redirects are followed.
Maximum number of redirects to follow. Default is unlimited. Useful for preventing redirect loops.
Preserves the original HTTP method when following redirects. By default, redirects are followed with GET method.
Removes the Referer header when following redirects. By default, Referer header is sent with redirects.
Number of retry attempts for failed requests (4xx, 5xx, network errors). Useful for flaky endpoints or rate-limited APIs. (default: 0)
Delay between retry attempts in milliseconds. (default: 1000)

Add JavaScript script to run before the request. Can be inline JS or @filepath to load from file. Scripts have access to pm.* APIs. Example: --script-pre-request "pm.environment.set('timestamp', Date.now());" or --script-pre-request @setup.js

Add JavaScript script to run after the request. Can be inline JS or @filepath to load from file. Scripts have access to pm.* APIs. Example: --script-post-request "console.log(pm.response.json());" or --script-post-request @validate.js
[BETA] Pass --dataset multiple times to load several .dataset.yaml files (or directories containing them). Each is made available to pre/post-request scripts as pm.datasets(<id>). (default: [])
Save complete response to JSON file.Useful for debugging or further processing.
Show detailed request and response information including headers, body, and metadata.
suppress all output except response body. Useful for piping to other commands.
Debug mode: show detailed execution information including retry attempts, redirects, and timing breakdowns.

commands.name: sdk
commands.description: Generate and manage your SDKs directly from the command line.

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Create or update .postman/config.json with an SDK configuration stub.

flags.description: Skip prompts and overwrite existing sdk config

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Generate a new SDK from a Postman collection or specification.

flags.description: Target language(s) for SDK generation. Specify one or more languages. [choices: "typescript", "python", "java", "go", "csharp", "php", "ruby", "cli", "kotlin", "rust"]
Generate SDKs for all supported languages
Directory where the generated SDK will be saved (default: "./sdks")
Name for the generated SDK package
Version number for the generated SDK
Path to SDK generation config file (JSON). If not specified, will look for .postman/config.json (sdk key).
Skip all confirmation prompts and overwrite existing files
Show detailed generation progress and logs
Set build log detail level. "warn" (default) shows errors/warnings inline and collapses info notes; "info" expands all log entries (default: "warn")
Disable change tracking. By default, file hashes are written into .manifest.json to enable edit detection on the next regeneration.
Skip custom code merge even if user edits are detected. Forces a fresh generation.
Strategy for handling merge conflicts: "mark" inserts conflict markers (default), "ours" keeps user edits, "theirs" keeps generated code
Skip downloading and saving SDK artifacts to disk. Useful in CI when the build only needs to trigger server-side pull requests.

subcommands.name: track
subcommands.description: Enable change tracking for a generated SDK. Adds file hashes to .manifest.json so that the next `sdk generate` can detect your edits and preserve them via 3-way merge.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List SDK builds for a collection, extensible collection, or specification.

flags.description: Filter builds by status (case-insensitive) [choices: in-progress, success, failure]
Set build log detail level. "warn" (default) shows errors/warnings inline and collapses info notes; "info" expands all log entries (default: "warn")

subcommands.name: fetch
subcommands.description: Download SDKs from a build by its ID.

flags.description: Directory to save the downloaded SDK(s) (default: "./sdks")
Download only the specified language (e.g., typescript, python)
Set build log detail level. "warn" (default) shows errors/warnings inline and collapses info notes; "info" expands all log entries (default: "warn")

subcommands.name: connect
subcommands.description: Connect a collection or specification to a GitHub repository for automatic SDK pull requests

flags.description: SDK language (required) [choices: typescript, python, java, go, csharp, php, ruby, cli, kotlin, rust]
Target branch for SDK pull requests (default: "main")
Push SDK updates directly to the base branch instead of opening a pull request (default: false)
Open auto-update pull requests when the SDK changes (true|false). Defaults to true server-side when omitted
OAuth provider used by the server when pushing (choices: githubapp, github) (default: "githubapp")

subcommands.name: connections
subcommands.description: Manage GitHub connections that drive automatic SDK pull requests

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List GitHub connections for a collection or specification

flags.description: Refresh PR statuses from GitHub before listing (slower) (default: false)
Output raw JSON instead of formatted text (default: false)

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a GitHub connection for a collection or specification

commands.name: mock
commands.description: Run and manage local Postman mock servers.

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Start a local mock server given a manifest file.

flags.description: Path to an environment file (JSON or YAML) for pm.environment
Path to a globals file (JSON or YAML) for pm.globals

commands.name: application
commands.description: Application-level commands: initialise config and run tests.

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialise a postman.config.cjs file with target collections, environment, and test command.

flags.description: Collection name or path for the default target (repeatable) (default: [])
Environment name or path for the default target (last value wins) (default: [])
Comma-separated URL patterns to exclude from captured traffic (default: [])
Comma-separated HTTP methods to exclude from captured traffic (default: [])
Comma-separated request header filters in header=value form (default: [])
Command to run your tests (e.g. "npx playwright test")

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Run a command first (primary task), then match captured traffic against Postman collections and run assertions.

flags.description: Select a target from postman.config.js targets block (default: "default")
Collection override for the selected target (repeatable) (default: [])
Environment override for the selected target (last value wins) (default: [])
Command to run first (primary task). Overrides config.

Directory to scan for captured artifacts, or a direct path to a Playwright JSON report / NDJSON capture file. When a directory is given, recursively discovers `*.json` Playwright reports plus their `trace.zip` attachments, and `network-capture-*.ndjson` at the top level. By default, scans Playwright config `outputDir`, test-results/, then pm-results/.
Skip collection matching and tests; capture traffic and save a V3 collection organised by host
Output directory for captured collection (default: pm-results/captured/; used with --capture-only)
Overwrite the output directory if it exists and is not empty (used with --capture-only)
Send application test run results and analytics to Postman
Show detailed per-request matching, sandbox errors, and debug information

commands.description: Start mock servers with simulated failure scenarios.

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Start mocks with fault-injection scenarios defined in a .sim.yaml file

Output format: default (card list), json (detailed JSON) (default: "default")

Filter as raw JSON. Only a top-level "$and" list of condition objects is supported. "$or" and nested groups are not supported in --filter-json. Each condition: { "<property>": { "<operator>": <value> } }. If both --filter and --filter-json are provided, --filter-json takes precedence.

Positional parameter values for $1, $2, ... bindings; repeatable

flags.description: Logical name for the source (becomes the SQL table name)
Source kind; inferred from --file/--url when omitted
Path to a local CSV/JSON file (absolute or cwd-relative)
Reference the file in place; do NOT copy into data_dir
When copying, overwrite an existing file at the destination
HTTP(S) URL to a remote CSV/JSON file
DB user (warning: stored as plaintext in the YAML).

When copying with --file, overwrite existing destination
Update DB user (warning: stored as plaintext in the YAML).
Update DB password (warning: stored as plaintext in the YAML).
